title: Product Launch Email Sequence
prompt:
You are an email marketing strategist. Write a five-email launch sequence for [product name]. Audience: [target audience]. Tone: clear and confident, not hype-driven.

Email 1: Teaser — introduce the problem and hint at a solution.
Email 2: Story — share why we built this and who it helps.
Email 3: Features — three concrete benefits with examples.
Email 4: Social proof — customer quote and one metric.
Email 5: Last chance — direct CTA with a deadline.

Keep each email under 200 words. Subject lines under 50 characters.

title: Technical Blog Post Outline
prompt:
Create a detailed outline for a technical blog post about [topic]. Target reader: [seniority level] engineers.

Include:
1. Hook — a specific problem the reader recognizes
2. Context — why this matters now
3. Core explanation — break into 3–4 sections with subheadings
4. Code example placeholder — what to demonstrate
5. Trade-offs — when not to use this approach
6. Conclusion — one actionable takeaway

Suggest a working title and meta description under 160 characters.

title: User Interview Script
prompt:
You are a UX researcher preparing a 30-minute user interview about [product area]. Participant profile: [role and context].

Write an interview guide with:
- 2 warm-up questions (non-leading)
- 5 discovery questions about current workflow and pain points
- 3 follow-up probes for each main question
- 1 closing question: "What would make this a no-brainer for you?"

Avoid yes/no questions. Do not mention our solution until the final 5 minutes.

title: Sprint Retrospective Facilitator
prompt:
Facilitate a 45-minute sprint retrospective for a team of [team size]. Sprint context: [what shipped, what slipped].

Structure:
1. Set the stage (5 min) — goal and norms
2. Gather data (10 min) — prompts for timeline review
3. Generate insights (15 min) — group themes, dot-vote top 3
4. Decide actions (10 min) — max 2 concrete improvements
5. Close (5 min) — recap owners and deadlines

Provide exact facilitator scripts and time-box reminders.

title: Landing Page Hero Copy
prompt:
Write hero section copy for a [product type] landing page. Company: [name]. Primary audience: [audience]. Main differentiator: [differentiator].

Deliver:
- Headline (max 10 words)
- Subheadline (max 25 words)
- Primary CTA button text
- Secondary CTA text

Tone: professional and direct. No buzzwords like "revolutionary" or "cutting-edge." Focus on the outcome the user gets.

title: Pull Request Description
prompt:
Write a pull request description for the following change: [describe change].

Use this structure:
## Summary
One paragraph explaining what and why.

## Changes
Bullet list of specific modifications.

## Testing
How this was verified.

## Screenshots
Placeholder note if UI changed.

Keep it scannable. Link related issue if applicable: [issue number].

title: Design Critique Framework
prompt:
Provide structured feedback on a [screen type] design for [product]. Context: [user goal and constraints].

Evaluate across:
1. Clarity — is the primary action obvious?
2. Hierarchy — does visual weight match importance?
3. Consistency — alignment with common patterns
4. Accessibility — contrast, touch targets, labels
5. Edge cases — empty states, errors, loading

For each area: one strength, one improvement, one specific suggestion. Be constructive, not vague.

title: Weekly Planning Template
prompt:
Help me plan my work week. Role: [role]. Top priorities this quarter: [priorities]. Meetings already scheduled: [list or estimate].

Output:
- 3 outcomes for the week (measurable)
- Daily focus blocks (morning deep work, afternoon meetings)
- One thing to defer with a reason
- Friday review prompts (what shipped, what blocked, what to adjust)

Keep total planned work at 80% capacity to leave buffer.

title: Competitive Analysis Brief
prompt:
Analyze [competitor name] relative to [our product]. Focus area: [feature or market segment].

Cover:
1. Positioning — how they describe themselves
2. Target customer — who they serve best
3. Strengths — 3 genuine advantages
4. Weaknesses — 3 gaps or complaints
5. Pricing model — summary
6. Opportunity — where we can differentiate

Use publicly available information only. Cite assumptions clearly.

title: Newsletter Edition Planner
prompt:
Plan one edition of a newsletter for [audience]. Topic focus: [theme]. Brand voice: [describe tone].

Include:
- Subject line (3 options, under 45 chars)
- Opening hook (2 sentences)
- Main story (3 paragraphs)
- One curated link with commentary
- Closing CTA

Total length: 400–500 words. Write for skimmers — use short paragraphs and one subheading.

title: API Documentation Draft
prompt:
Write documentation for an API endpoint.

Endpoint: [method] [path]
Purpose: [what it does]
Auth: [requirements]
Request body: [schema or example]
Response: [schema or example]

Structure:
- Overview (1 sentence)
- Request parameters table
- Example request (curl)
- Example response (JSON)
- Error codes with meanings
- Rate limits if applicable

Write for a developer seeing this endpoint for the first time.

title: Social Media Campaign Calendar
prompt:
Create a two-week social media content calendar for [brand/product]. Campaign goal: [goal]. Platforms: [list platforms].

For each post provide:
- Day and platform
- Post format (text, image, thread)
- Copy draft
- Hashtags (max 3, relevant only)
- Goal (awareness, engagement, traffic)

Balance promotional and value content 30/70. No engagement bait.
